pqs.question: Has the State promulgated specific operating regulations to transpose the provisions of Annex 1?
pqs.lead: Confirm transposition and verify regulations for:
criteria: Confirm the title, date of promulgation and the latest amendment of all regulations related to personnel licensing.
Verify that the contents of the regulations are consistent, sufficient and properly organized.
Confirm the transposition of the personnel licensing related provisions for all licences and ratings, including remote pilot licences.
Confirm the transposition of the personnel licensing related provisions for medical assessment.
Confirm the transposition of the personnel licensing related provisions for approval of training organizations.
Confirm the transposition of the personnel licensing related provisions for qualification/approval of flight simulation training devices.
Confirm the transposition of the personnel licensing related provisions for language used for radiotelephony communications (i.e. language proficiency requirements).

pqs.question: In addition to the promulgated regulations, has the Licensing Authority issued orders, directives and/or instructions in support of its personnel licensing activities?
criteria: Confirm the title, content and date of issuance of the last amendment to orders, directives and instructions.
Verify approval process and enforceability.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for amending its specific PEL regulations, taking into consideration ICAO provisions and their amendments?
pqs.note: The establishment of amendment procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.009.
criteria: Review documented evidence of effective implementation of procedures for amending PEL regulations.
Verify that regulations are amended in a timely manner whenever amendments to Annex 1 are received.
Verify the action taken by the State after receipt of the latest amendments to Annex 1.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for identifying and notifying differences if any, to ICAO?
pqs.note: The establishment of these procedures is addressed in LEG PQ 1.025.
criteria: Review documented evidence of effective implementation.

Confirm that the differences between the SARPs and the State legislation and practices are identified and notified to ICAO using the Compliance Checklist/Electronic Filing of Differences (CC/EFOD) system.
Confirm that significant differences between SARPs/PANS/SUPPS and the State regulations and practices are identified and published in the AIP.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for granting exemptions in the area of personnel licensing?
pqs.note: The term 'exemptions' also includes exceptions, deviations, variations and prolonged extensions. Legal basis under LEG 1.027 and 1.028.
criteria: Verify implementation of procedures for recording and publishing exemptions granted.
Review examples of exemptions granted in the area of personnel licensing.
Verify implementation of the risk assessment system before granting of exemption.

pqs.question: Has the State established an organizational structure for personnel licensing?
pqs.note: This PQ is not linked to ORG PQ 2.010.
criteria: Evaluate current approved organizational structure for CAA and personnel licensing office, including lines of responsibility.

pqs.question: Are the functions and responsibilities of the personnel licensing office clearly defined?
criteria: Review the document detailing functions and responsibilities of the PEL Office.

pqs.question: Have job descriptions been established for technical personnel and key management personnel of the personnel licensing office?
criteria: Review job descriptions for all personnel licensing staff.
Check the job description including all tasks related to Annex 1 (e.g. licensing, medical, training organizations approval and surveillance).

pqs.question: Does the personnel licensing office have sufficient human resources to carry out its functions and mandate?
pqs.lead: Verify staffing capacity for baseline oversight tasks, including:
criteria: Review methodology established for determining staffing needs, if available.
Review the ability to attract new officers as well as existing vacancies and level of turnover in the past years.
Review ability to carry out reviewing and revising regulations.
Review ability to carry out training of technical staff.
Review ability to carry out development of guidance material.
Review ability to carry out issuance of licences and rating.
Review ability to carry out conducting surveillance.
Review ability to carry out resolving identified safety concerns.

pqs.question: Has the State established appropriate minimum qualification and experience requirements for its personnel licensing staff?
pqs.lead: Review deployment requirements based on technical tasks:
criteria: Review qualification and experience requirements for recruitment and assignment of personnel licensing staff of the Licensing Authority depending on their responsibilities in the personnel licensing issuance process and oversight system.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that all personnel licensing staff meet the established minimum qualification and experience requirements?
criteria: Sample recruitment files.
Cross-check with established requirements.

pqs.question: Is a formal training programme established that details the type of training that should be provided to personnel licensing inspectorate staff and other technical personnel?
pqs.lead: Verify structured training specifications, confirming inclusion of:
criteria: Review contents of training programme for each job (inspectorate staff and other technical personnel).
Review contents of training programme for all activities (i.e. issuance of all licences, medical, training organizations approval).
Initial training modules.
On-the-job training (OJT) structures.
Recurrent training cycles.
Specialized training with timelines to be provided, as applicable.

pqs.question: Have personnel licensing staff been issued credentials to facilitate access to aircraft, aviation facilities and documents for the purpose of inspections and enforcement?
pqs.note: This PQ is only applicable to those performing inspection and enforcement activities.
criteria: Reference to empowering legislation.
Method established to control currency of credential.
Inspector's photo.

pqs.question: Has a periodic training plan been established and implemented, which details and prioritizes the type of training that will be provided to personnel licensing inspectorate staff and other technical personnel during the established period?
pqs.lead: Verify compliance with training continuity profiles:
criteria: Review the most recent training plan for each inspectorate staff and other technical personnel.
Check training files.
Verify that the completed training (initial, recurrent, specialized and OJT) is sufficient for staff to acquire/maintain knowledge.
The training plan should reflect the schedule for implementing the training programme in the following year(s).

pqs.question: Has a system been established to maintain the training records of personnel licensing staff?
criteria: Review requirements/instructions for the establishment and maintenance of training records.
Verify that the system in place complies with system description.
Verify that training records are systematically retained.

pqs.question: Are the relevant ICAO documents and other technical publications readily available to the personnel licensing inspectorate staff and other technical personnel?
pqs.note: Check for field/regional offices as well as headquarters.
pqs.lead: Verify publication library tracks and distribution controls for:
criteria: Evaluate method to ensure receipt, control and distribution of the necessary technical documentation.
Evaluate method to determine currency of documents.
Verify accessibility of up-to-date State laws and regulations.
Verify accessibility of orders and instructions.
Verify accessibility of current copy of Annex 1.
Verify accessibility of copies of ICAO guidance material (i.e. Doc 9379, Doc 9841, Doc 9625, etc.).

pqs.question: Is there a technical library available for the staff of the personnel licensing office or another method to ensure receipt, control and distribution of the necessary technical documentation?
criteria: Evaluate document receipt, control, and distribution system.
Evaluate method to determine currency of documents.

pqs.question: Are personnel licensing staff provided with comprehensive guidance material and procedures that are sufficiently detailed to enable them to carry out their functions and responsibilities effectively?
criteria: Review the PEL Procedures Manual, if available, or the list of procedures available.
Review management process.
Review distribution process of the documents used to ensure that all staff use comprehensive and up-to-date documents.

pqs.question: Has the State established methods to inform applicants of the requirements to be met for the various licences and ratings and the documents to be submitted for the application process?
criteria: Review available methods for informing applicants of the different licences and ratings.
Review list of application forms for all types of licences, ratings or examinations.
Review the forms to ensure that they contain information relating to age, knowledge, experience, skills and medical information of the applicant.

pqs.question: Does the State implement a process for the systematic review and evaluation of submitted application forms for the issuance of licences and ratings and for examinations?
pqs.note: Applies to all types of licences, ratings and examinations.
criteria: Review implementation of processes and procedures for the evaluation of applications for licences, ratings or examinations.
Review responsibilities of PEL staff in assessing documents provided by applicants (e.g. different steps, who is doing what).
Sample cases for review.

pqs.question: Has the State established a procedure for appealing the Licensing Authority’s decisions?
criteria: Review documented description of the appeal process.
Review established and documented processes and procedures.
Review documented procedure used to inform the applicant of the appeal process for licences, ratings, examinations and medical decisions.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations to ensure that privileges granted by a pilot licence, or by related ratings, are not exercised unless the holder maintains competency and meets requirements for recency of experience?
criteria: Verify regulations for all pilot licences and ratings issued, including instructor rating (e.g. list, conditions of issuance and renewal, maintenance of competency, recency of experience, etc.).
Ensure that such requirements also apply if a pilot is not employed by a commercial air operator.

pqs.question: Has the State established a system to re-establish or reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings?
pqs.note: The implementation of a system to re-establish or reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings is addressed in PEL PQ 3.257 (CE-6).
pqs.lead: Verify policy configuration for privilege restoration:
criteria: Review documented requirements (e.g. number of hours of mandatory re-training, examinations, etc.) to reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings.
Review documented process and procedures to reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system to re-establish or reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings?
pqs.note: The establishment of this system is addressed in PEL PQ 3.256 (CE-5).
pqs.lead: Verify action implementation for privilege restoration:
criteria: Review implementation of requirements (e.g. number of hours of mandatory re-training, examinations, etc.) to reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings.
Review implementation of procedure to reinstate the privileges of expired licences and ratings.
Sample cases to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: If the State issues civil licences to military pilots, has a system been implemented for issuing civil pilot licences and ratings and granting exemptions?
pqs.note: The establishment of a policy and procedures for granting exemptions is addressed in LEG PQ 1.028.
criteria: Review the rules and procedures followed for the conversion of military qualifications and experience to equivalent civil licences and ratings.
Sample cases for review to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State systematically implemented a process for the renewal of licences and ratings?
criteria: Review procedures and conditions for the renewal of licences issued.
Review the competency requirements such as recent experience requirements, recurrent training and testing.
Confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that a pilot licence holder has not performed his/her duties in accordance with the prescribed procedures and privileges of his/her licence?
criteria: Review enforcement system or procedures.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation of a graduated response system (warnings, sanctions).

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for the issuance of pilot licences and ratings on the basis of foreign licences (licence conversion)?
criteria: Review the established procedures for the conversion of a foreign licence.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for the conversion/validation of all type of licences, other than pilot licences?
pqs.lead: Verify baseline codes for alternative licenses:
criteria: Verify regulations and additional requirements for conversion/validation of licences other than pilot licences.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for the validation of foreign pilot licences and ratings?
criteria: Review the established procedures for the validation of a foreign licence.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for the conversion/validation of the commercial pilot, multi-crew pilot and airline transport pilot licences that are used for air transport purposes?
criteria: Verify regulations and additional requirements for the conversion/validation of these commercial and airline transport licences.

pqs.question: Does the Licensing Authority contact the foreign licence issuing State systematically to ensure the validity of the licence to be converted or validated?
pqs.note: Contacting the issuing State is the best methodology, but other processes and procedures may be available.
criteria: Review implementation of process and procedures for confirmation of the validity of the foreign licence.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation for all types of licences.

pqs.question: Does the State have a system to ensure that the original licence submitted for a licence validation or conversion is in full compliance with Annex 1?
pqs.lead: Verify compliance audit logs for external files:
criteria: Review implementation of process and procedures for confirmation of the licence’s conformity with requirements of Annex 1.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation for all types of licences.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a systematic process to answer queries from other States in order to confirm the validity of the licences issued?
criteria: Review process and procedures established and implemented for answering queries from other States.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation for all types of licences.

pqs.question: Have procedures been implemented for the validation/conversion of foreign licences and ratings of aircraft maintenance technicians/engineers/mechanics?
pqs.note: Applicable only if such validation/conversion is performed in the State.
criteria: Review validation/conversion procedures for aircraft maintenance personnel.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Have procedures been implemented for the validation/conversion of foreign licences of flight operations officers/flight dispatchers?
pqs.note: Applicable only if such validation/conversion is performed in the State.
criteria: Review validation/conversion procedures for flight operations officers/flight dispatchers.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Have procedures been implemented for the validation/conversion of foreign licences and ratings of air traffic controllers?
pqs.note: Applicable only if such validation/conversion is performed in the State.
criteria: Review validation/conversion procedures for air traffic controllers.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Have procedures been established and implemented for issue/validation/conversion of foreign licences and ratings for remote pilot licences?
pqs.note: Applicable only if such issuance/validation/conversion is performed in the State.
criteria: Review procedures for issue/validation/conversion of remote pilot licences.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Have procedures been established and implemented for automatic validation of licences?
pqs.note: Applicable only if such issue/validation/conversion is performed in the State.
criteria: Review automatic validation procedures.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for medical examinations of applicants of various licences by medical examiners?
criteria: Verify regulations regarding medical provisions for licensing, including designation of medical examiners and requirement of a medical assessor.
Review regulations for the applicant to sign and furnish to medical examiners, a declaration, whose content conforms to the requirements of Annex 1.
Check promulgation of latest amendments to Annex 1.

pqs.question: Does the State use the services of medical assessors to evaluate reports submitted to the Licensing Authority by designated medical examiners (DME)?
pqs.note: Alternative systems can be acceptable, but they must guarantee evaluation of medical reports and effective oversight of all DMEs, medical centres/facilities.
criteria: Qualifications, experience, functions and responsibility of the medical assessor must be clearly described.
Review the process and documented evidence of the appointment of the medical assessor.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process for the evaluation of medical reports submitted by the designated medical examiners (DME)?
pqs.lead: Verify check routines for incoming medical forms:
criteria: Verify that the process and methods in place for the evaluation of medical reports comply with Docs 8984 and 9379.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation, e.g. audit reports of medical assessments and analysis of medical outcomes.

pqs.question: Has the State established the appropriate actions to be taken in case of a false declaration by an applicant to a designated medical examiner (DME)?
criteria: Review procedures outlining actions to be taken.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation, i.e. reports and evidence of a graduated response system (warnings, sanctions, etc. that have been issued).

pqs.question: Do designated medical examiners (DME) systematically submit signed reports to the Licensing Authority after completing the medical examination of an applicant?
pqs.note: Detailed medical information must be available only through the medical section or medical assessor to ensure medical confidentiality.
pqs.lead: Verify submission loops for DME logs:
criteria: If the medical examination is carried out by 2 or more examiners, verify that an appointed examiner is responsible for coordinating the results and signing of the report.
If reports are submitted electronically, verify that adequate identification of the examiner has been established.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation, i.e. records of submission and acknowledgement of receipt.
Verify that all reports are available at the Licensing Authority for proper review and analysis by an appropriately qualified and experienced medical personnel.

pqs.question: Does the Licensing Authority perform medical assessment audits?
pqs.note: The purpose of such audits is to ensure that DMEs meet applicable standards for good medical practice and aeromedical risk assessment.
criteria: Confirm that audit process is established.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation, i.e. audit reports.

pqs.question: Has the Licensing Authority implemented a system to ensure the confidentiality of medical reports and records at all times?
criteria: Ensure the Licensing Authority checks the confidentiality system at designated medical examiners’ (DME) facilities or/and medical centres.
Check confidentiality system of the reports kept by Licensing Authority.
Check the policy and procedures established to determine to what extent pertinent medical information is presented to relevant officials of the Licensing Authority and other stakeholders.
Review reports/complaints for any breach of medical confidentiality.

pqs.question: If the medical Standards prescribed in Chapter 6 of Annex 1 for a particular licence type are not fully met, has the State implemented a system for the possible issuance or renewal of the appropriate medical assessment following conditions as stipulated in Annex 1?
criteria: Confirm that accredited medical conclusion indicates that exercise of privileges is not likely to jeopardize flight safety under special circumstances.
Confirm relevant ability, skills and experience of the applicant and operational conditions have been given due consideration.
Confirm the licence is endorsed with any special limitation or limitations when safe performance is dependent on compliance.
Confirm that procedures for coordination between medical assessors, other subject matter experts (SMEs) and Licensing Authority are established.
Review minutes of medical review/board meetings to confirm consultation with SMEs, operations department, etc.
Review reports including analysis of loss of licence due to medical reasons and trend analysis of findings of the outcome of the medical certification.
Review policy, procedures and evidence of communication for the justification and outcome of the medical certification decision.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process describing circumstances under which a medical examination may be deferred?
pqs.note: If the policy formally adopted by the State is that the medical examination cannot be deferred, the PQ is “Not Applicable”.
criteria: Confirm that the process developed and implemented complies with the regulations.
If deferred, review the frequency and reasons for deferral.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes its national licence holders to perform their medical examinations in a foreign State, has a system been implemented to ensure proper designation and surveillance?
criteria: Check if overseas DMEs are properly designated, trained and in good standing with the overseas Licensing Authority.
Check the system for receiving and assessing medical reports carried out by the foreign authority.
Review evidence to ensure effective implementation via list of foreign DMEs and audit reports.

pqs.question: If the State has not established any system for assessing the medical fitness of licence applicants, has it implemented practices to ensure medical fitness via validation?
criteria: Evaluate procedures and practices implemented for the validation or conversion of foreign medical assessments.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation, e.g. number of foreign validations and trend analysis of medical findings.

pqs.question: Has the State established a procedure for the designation of medical examiners and provided guidance material to them?
criteria: Check conditions and duration of the designation and scope of authority given (e.g. issuance of waivers, classes, etc.).
Check guidelines provided to the medical examiners (e.g. template reports, manual for the medical assessment, etc.).
Check methods for informing medical examiners of new medical-related provisions.

pqs.question: Does the State require designated medical examiners (DMEs) to demonstrate their competence in aviation medicine before designation?
criteria: Verify that, before initial designation, the DMEs undergo required training and demonstrate adequate competency in aviation medicine.
Verify that DMEs have practical knowledge and experience of the conditions in which licence holders carry out duties (flight/simulator observation).
Review evidence via publication of DME list and file reviews.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that designated medical examiners (DMEs) attend refresher training in aviation medicine at regular intervals?
criteria: Review procedures for renewal of designation.
Verify that, before the renewal of designation, the DMEs undergo refresher training within intervals of 3 to 5 years.
Sample DME training files to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system for the supervision and control of designated medical examiners (DME)?
criteria: Verify inclusion of premises and equipment inspections in the DME oversight system.
Verify demonstration of use of latest ICAO SARPs and up-to-date refresher training.
Verify timely transmittal of reports to the Licensing Authority’s medical department.

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that a designated medical examiner (DME) has not performed his/her duties in accordance with the prescribed procedures?
criteria: Review enforcement system and/or procedures.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation of a graduated response system (warnings, sanctions).

pqs.question: Has the State implemented appropriate aviation-related health promotion for licence holders subject to a medical assessment?
criteria: Verify analysis of in-flight incapacitation events and findings during medical assessments.
Review list of appropriate topics identified for health promotion and communication with stakeholders.

pqs.question: If the Licensing Authority develops, conducts and/or corrects its own written and oral examinations for the issuance of all or some licences and ratings by the State, has the State implemented an appropriate and comprehensive system?
criteria: Check if guidance to examiners on how to prepare examinations has been developed.
Check if there are procedures for developing and revising questionnaires periodically using a question pool.
Review evidence to confirm implementation for all types of exams organized (pilots, ATCOs, aircraft maintenance engineers).

pqs.question: If the Licensing Authority issues licences and ratings by using written and oral examinations that it did not develop and/or conduct and/or correct, has the State implemented a system to ensure consistency?
pqs.note: This PQ is also APPLICABLE if the State accepts results of examinations taken in a foreign State by its nationals.
criteria: Check that there is a formal system implemented concerning delegation for the development/conduct/correction of examinations.
Check that there is a system for the surveillance of delegated persons or organizations.

pqs.question: Has the State established appropriate minimum qualification requirements for examiners who prepare questions or assess examinations?
pqs.note: This PQ is also APPLICABLE for examiners if some part of the examination process is delegated.
criteria: Verify the document/procedures detailing the knowledge and experience requirement for designation as an examiner for pilots, ATCOs, and maintenance engineers.
Review existing examiner's records to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process for the designation of examiners for written and oral exams?
criteria: Review process for the selection, designation and appointment of examiners for pilots, ATCOs, and maintenance engineers.
Review list of examiners, including specialty and type of exam authorized to prepare and conduct.
Check the implementation of the system for their designation (official letter, duration, etc.).

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a comprehensive system for the designation of flight and practical examiners?
pqs.note: Applicable also for practical examiners for air traffic controller and aircraft maintenance engineer licences.
criteria: Verify the requirements for the designation and qualifications of flight and practical examiners.
Review procedures for designation, duration of designation, and defined duties/responsibilities.
Sample designated examiner files and records to confirm implementation.
Review the system implemented for flight and practical examinations to ensure applicants meet knowledge and skill requirements specified for Annex 1.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system for the supervision and control of flight and practical test delivery, which ensures consistency and reliability of testing by the designated flight and practical examiners?
pqs.note: Applicable also for practical examiners for air traffic controller and aircraft maintenance engineer licences.
criteria: Evaluate system for supervision by the authority of the designated flight and practical examiners while performing functions.
Review frequency of surveillance established.
Verify guidance material for examiners and/or post assessment by the Licensing Authority.

pqs.question: How does the State ensure the maintenance of qualification and proficiency of flight and practical examiners?
criteria: Verify recurrent training rules for flight and practical examiners.
Verify conditions for renewal of designated flight and practical examiners.
Verify supervision done by the Licensing Authority.

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that a flight or practical examiner has not performed his/her duties in accordance with the prescribed procedures?
pqs.note: Applicable also for practical examiners for air traffic controller and aircraft maintenance engineer licences.
criteria: Review enforcement procedures.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation of a graduated response system (warnings, sanctions).

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system for formally evaluating and re-evaluating language proficiency at the level specified in Appendix 1 to Annex 1?
pqs.note: Applies to pilots (aeroplane, airship, helicopter, powered-lift), flight navigators using radiotelephone, ATCOs, and aeronautical station operators.
criteria: Verify description and references of the system implemented to formally evaluate and/or re-evaluate language proficiency.
Check the system for nominating/designating the person or organization performing the evaluation.
Review the contents of the tests and ensure that intervals for re-evaluation conform with ICAO guidance.
Check implementation of the system for endorsement of language proficiency ratings in licences (validity date, Level 4 or higher status, failure handling).

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the process and quality of the language proficiency system conform with the provisions of Annex 1 and related guidance materials?
pqs.lead: Verify LPR system quality coverage across categories:
criteria: Verify regulations promulgated and related guidance implemented for pilots, navigators, ATCOs, station operators, flight engineers, glider pilots, and free balloon pilots.

pqs.question: Does the State maintain a personal file for each applicant and licence holder containing progressive history and current status?
criteria: Evaluate filing system to ensure each file contains all correspondence, applications, assessments, exam results, and licensing documentation.
Review filing procedures to confirm effective implementation and progressive tracking of qualifications.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system to ensure confidentiality and security of individual records and for closing and archiving personal files?
criteria: Review filing procedures and evaluate system for maintaining confidentiality and control of access.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented procedures for the approval of domestic and foreign aviation training organizations (TOs)?
criteria: Check procedures for approval of domestic and foreign TOs covering pilot, ATCO, and maintenance TOs.
Check training organization administered by authority, if applicable.
Check if procedures cover approval of training programmes, review of the content of the training and procedures manual, quality assurance system, and qualification of synthetic training devices.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a system to ensure the qualification and competency of the instructors of approved training organizations (ATOs)?
criteria: Review evidence to confirm implementation of State’s procedures to ensure qualifications and competence of instructional personnel of ATOs (for pilots, ATCOs, and maintenance engineers).
Check ATOs administered by authority, if applicable.

pqs.question: If aviation training organizations (TOs) conduct examinations and testing on behalf of the Licensing Authority, has the Licensing Authority implemented a system to ensure proper surveillance?
criteria: Review legal basis of delegation and agreement between the Licensing Authority and TOs.
Review methods used to prevent conflict of interest within domestic and foreign TOs.
Review effective implementation of the system used for qualification and oversight of examiners as part of the TO approval package.

pqs.question: Does the State issue a training organization’s (TO) approval document, which is comprehensive and contains at least name, location, date, validity, and terms?
criteria: Verify approval certificate layout of TO for pilots, air traffic controllers, aircraft maintenance engineers, as applicable.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the approved training organization (TO) develops and publishes a training and procedures manual for the use of personnel concerned?
criteria: Review implementation of procedure or guidelines with respect to the content of the training and procedures manual conforming to Annex 1, Appendix 2.
Check if the manuals of TOs are reviewed and approved by the Licensing Authority.

pqs.question: Does the process of issuing an approval to a training organization (TO) take into account pertinent aspects related to facilities, equipment, key personnel and organization?
criteria: Facilities, working environment and installations (information, equipment, training devices and material).
Qualification of synthetic training devices in accordance with State’s regulations (Doc 9625).
Vetting of key personnel (including instructors and examiners), training records, evaluation/checking, and quality management system.

pqs.question: Does the State implement an effective surveillance programme of the approved training organizations (ATOs) to ensure continuing compliance?
criteria: Evaluate procedures for conduct of surveillance programme of domestic and foreign approved ATOs.
Review methods of oversight and surveillance programme records of the previous year.
Confirm appropriate frequency of inspections (planned or random inspections).

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that a training organization has not performed its function according to its procedures manual?
criteria: Review enforcement system and/or procedures for domestic and foreign TOs.
Review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: If the State authorizes/approves foreign training organizations (TOs) to provide approved training to its licence holders, does it implement a system for approval/validation?
criteria: Review list of foreign TOs which provide training to the State’s licence holders.
Verify if the State applies the same system of approval as used for domestic TOs or implements a process of validation/acceptance of foreign approvals.
Check the validation system and supplementary conditions established (oversight capability check of issuing State, copy of approvals for TO and simulators, check examiners used, etc.).

pqs.question: Does the State implement a process to approve training programmes related to the first issuance of licences and ratings?
pqs.note: If automatically validating, check common licensing system within an RSOO and Article 83 agreements.
criteria: Check the implementation of process for approval of training programmes (domestic or foreign TOs).
Review evidence to confirm all amendments to training programmes are approved by the authority.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for training or licensing, as applicable, of aircraft maintenance technicians, engineers and mechanics?
criteria: Verify that regulations include all licences and rating requirements of aircraft maintenance technicians/engineers/mechanics.
Verify that regulations specify the conditions to be observed in exercising privileges.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process for the systematic review and validation of application forms from aircraft maintenance technicians/engineers/mechanics?
criteria: Review implementation of the process and procedures for the issuance, renewal, validation and extension of technician licences.
Sample files to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Have procedures been established to ensure that aircraft maintenance technicians/engineers/mechanics meet the conditions/qualifications for the type of licence applied for?
criteria: Review procedures to ensure they cover the validation of skills and requirements.

pqs.question: Does the State prescribe on the licence or in a separate document the scope of the privileges of the aircraft maintenance technician/engineer/mechanic licence holder?
criteria: Review scope of privileges mapped in terms of complexity of tasks related to certification.
Sample files to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Has the authority implemented a surveillance system of the aircraft maintenance technician/engineer/mechanic licences issued?
criteria: Review the establishment of the surveillance process and review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that an aircraft maintenance technician/engineer/mechanic has not performed his/her duties in accordance with procedures?
criteria: Review enforcement system or procedures and sample evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated regulations for training or licensing, as applicable, of air traffic controllers?
criteria: Verify that the regulations include all licence and ratings requirements of air traffic controllers.
Verify that regulations specify conditions to be observed in exercising privileges.

pqs.question: Have procedures been established for the issuance of air traffic controller licence and ratings and/or to ensure that requirements are met?
pqs.note: Unlicensed State employees may operate as ATCOs on condition that they meet same requirements (Annex 1, 4.4.1).
criteria: Review issuance procedures and verify if the licensing process is delegated to an entity or organization outside the Licensing Authority.

pqs.question: Has the State implemented a process for the systematic review and validation of applications from air traffic controllers for the issuance, renewal, validation and extension of licences and ratings?
criteria: Review implementation of the process and procedures, and sample files to confirm implementation.

pqs.question: Has the Licensing Authority implemented a surveillance system of air traffic controller licences issued?
criteria: Review evidence to confirm effective implementation of the surveillance process for air traffic controllers.

pqs.question: What action does the Licensing Authority take in the event that sufficient evidence exists to demonstrate that an air traffic controller has not performed his/her duties in accordance with prescribed procedures?
criteria: Review enforcement system or procedures and review evidence to confirm effective implementation.

pqs.question: Has the State promulgated specific operating regulations to require approved training organizations (ATOs) to implement a safety management system (SMS)?
pqs.note: Applicable only to approved training organizations (ATOs) exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
criteria: Require ATOs to implement an SMS that is acceptable to the State.
Require the SMS to be established in accordance with the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19.
Comply with the latest amendments to Annex 19.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the personnel responsible for the acceptance and continuous surveillance of ATOs SMS are qualified to perform their functions?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
pqs.lead: Verify safety management qualifications and records:
criteria: The State has identified competency requirements for personnel responsible for acceptance and surveillance.
The content of the training programme addresses the SMS framework elements in Appendix 2 to Annex 19.
A training plan has been implemented with initial, OJT, and recurrent training records systematically retained.

pqs.question: Has the State provided guidance material to the industry on the establishment and management of an SMS?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
criteria: Includes the processes for the establishment and management of an SMS.
Addresses the SMS framework elements contained in Appendix 2 to Annex 19.
Is updated, made accessible to ATOs, and corresponds with activities conducted by the ATOs.

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the acceptance of ATOs SMS?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
pqs.lead: Verify that the established procedures describe processes for acceptance and at least include:
criteria: Safety policy, safety objectives, regulatory SMS requirements, and system description.
Safety accountabilities, key safety personnel, and mandatory/voluntary safety reporting procedures.
Hazard identification, risk assessment, safety investigations, and performance indicator monitoring.
SMS training, safety communication, internal audits, management of change, documentation, and ERP coordination.

pqs.question: Has the State established procedures for the continuous surveillance of ATOs SMS?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
pqs.lead: Verify SMS continuous audit tracking lines:
criteria: Verify that the established procedures describe the processes for continuous surveillance of an SMS.
Are updated and made accessible to personnel responsible for surveillance.

pqs.question: Has the State accepted the SMS of individual ATOs?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
criteria: Review evidence of implemented procedures for accepting an SMS including the maintenance of SMS operational records.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the ATOs identify hazards, assess identified safety risks, and mitigate them appropriately?
pqs.note: This verification needs to be reviewed during the industry visit. Applicable to operational risk exposed ATOs.
pqs.lead: Verify that the State ensures that the ATOs maintain active processes to:
criteria: Identify hazards associated with their aviation products or services.
Assess safety risks and mitigate safety risks appropriately.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that safety performance indicators and targets established by ATOs are acceptable to the State?
pqs.note: SPIs acceptance may also be done later. Reviewed during industry visit. Applicable to operational risk exposed ATOs.
criteria: Review State’s mechanism for accepting individual ATO SPIs and targets to ensure they are specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, and timebound.
Verify that the State ensures that the ATOs proposed SPIs are reviewed and accepted as part of SMS acceptance.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure the ATOs monitor their safety performance?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
pqs.lead: Verify that the State has established and implemented a mechanism to ensure ATOs periodically validate:
criteria: The effectiveness of safety risks controls.
The identification of changes that may affect the level of safety risks associated with products or services.
The identification and management of safety risks that may arise from those changes.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the ATOs personnel involved in SMS are qualified to perform their functions and responsibilities?
pqs.note: Review training records during industry visit. Applicable to operational risk exposed ATOs.
pqs.lead: Verify training check routines for industry staff:
criteria: Verify that the State has established and implemented a mechanism to validate that ATO personnel involved in SMS are qualified.
Sample the surveillance activities records to confirm enforcement.

pqs.question: Does the State ensure that the ATOs continually promote safety?
pqs.note: Applicable to ATOs exposed to safety risks related to aircraft operations.
pqs.lead: Verify that the State ensures that the ATOs maintain formal means for:
criteria: Safety communication ensuring personnel are aware of the SMS to a degree commensurate with positions.
Conveying safety-critical information, explaining action rationales, and explaining procedure changes.
Considering communicating to external organizations any of their safety related information.
